channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: CREATIVE Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Clinical Research Training Opportunity in Africa (EU-Funded Programme)
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The Clinical ResEArch Training fellowship with Industry involVEment (CREATIVE Fellowship) is a prestigious, fully funded international programme designed to strengthen clinical research capacity across Sub-Saharan Africa. Funded by the European Union through Global Health EDCTP3, this fellowship offers a rare opportunity for early-career researchers to gain advanced academic training, practical research experience, and exposure to […]

The post CREATIVE Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Clinical Research Training Opportunity in Africa (EU-Funded Programme) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Clinical ResEArch Training fellowship with Industry involVEment (CREATIVE Fellowship) is a prestigious, fully funded international programme designed to strengthen clinical research capacity across Sub-Saharan Africa. Funded by the European Union through Global Health EDCTP3, this fellowship offers a rare opportunity for early-career researchers to gain advanced academic training, practical research experience, and exposure to the global health industry.

This multi-country initiative is structured to empower African researchers by equipping them with the knowledge, skills, and networks necessary to contribute meaningfully to health research and innovation on the continent and beyond.

Programme Overview

The CREATIVE Fellowship runs for a duration of 30 months and provides a comprehensive, multi-dimensional training experience. Fellows will be immersed in a rigorous programme that combines:

  • Academic advancement
  • Practical clinical research
  • Industry exposure
  • Professional mentorship

The fellowship is expected to begin in October 2026 and will be implemented across six African countries.

Key Components of the Fellowship

1. Work Placement at Leading Research Institutions

Fellows will be placed at one of six internationally recognized health research institutions in Africa. Each fellow will remain at their assigned institution throughout the fellowship, actively participating in impactful clinical research projects.

The host institutions include:

  • Haramaya University (Ethiopia)
  • Kintampo Health Research Centre, Ghana Health Service (Ghana)
  • Medical Research Council Unit, The Gambia
  • MRC/UVRI and LSHTM Uganda Research Unit (Uganda)
  • Zambart (Zambia)
  • Biomedical Research and Training Institute (Zimbabwe)

Each institution will host two fellows, making a total of 12 fellowship slots available.

2. Academic Training (Master’s Degree)

Participants will enroll in a Distance Learning MSc programme at the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine (LSHTM), UK. Fellows will choose between:

  • MSc in Clinical Trials (9 fellows)
  • MSc in Epidemiology (3 fellows)

The academic programme will be completed within the fellowship duration and is designed to complement the hands-on research experience.

3. Industry Exposure

Fellows will have the opportunity to apply for a competitive industry internship, allowing them to gain valuable insights into how clinical research operates within the private and global health sectors.

4. Structured Mentorship

A key strength of the CREATIVE Fellowship is its strong mentorship component. Fellows will receive guidance from experienced professionals who will support:

  • MSc completion
  • Career development
  • Networking opportunities
  • Transition into post-fellowship roles

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a national of Ethiopia, Ghana, The Gambia, Uganda, Zambia, or Zimbabwe
  • Apply for a fellowship hosted in their country of nationality
  • Hold a first- or second-class honours degree in a relevant field such as:
    • Natural sciences
    • Life sciences
    • Epidemiology
    • Statistics
    • Medicine or related disciplines
  • Have at least one year of experience in health research
  • Meet Band B English language requirements (test results may be submitted later if not available at application stage)
  • Be available to begin the fellowship full-time in October 2026
  • Provide two reference letters, including one from a current employer

Application Requirements

Applicants must complete an online application and upload the following documents:

  1. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  2. Academic transcripts
  3. Academic certificates
  4. Two reference letters
  5. Proof of nationality

Additionally, applicants must submit a personal statement addressing:

  • Motivation for applying
  • Academic and professional background
  • Expected outcomes from the fellowship
  • Career plans after completion
  • Preferred MSc programme (optional preference)

Applicants are encouraged to provide detailed and well-structured responses, as this plays a crucial role in the selection process.

Selection and Shortlisting Criteria

Applications will be evaluated based on:

  1. Academic merit (grades and qualifications)
  2. Relevant work experience
  3. Motivation and clarity of career goals

Only the most competitive candidates will be shortlisted for interviews.

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: 29 May 2026
  • Interview Period: July 2026
  • Programme Start: October 2026

Why You Should Apply

The CREATIVE Fellowship is more than just a scholarship—it is a career-transforming opportunity that bridges academia, research, and industry. Fellows gain:

  • A fully funded Master’s degree
  • Real-world clinical research experience
  • Access to global health networks
  • Career advancement opportunities

This programme is ideal for individuals passionate about advancing healthcare research and making a lasting impact in Africa.

Click here to learn more about CREATIVE Fellowship 2026

To discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, click Here

The post CREATIVE Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Clinical Research Training Opportunity in Africa (EU-Funded Programme) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WTO Fish Fund Extends Deadline to 8 May 2026: Expanded Opportunity for Developing Nations to Access Up to USD 300,000 in Grants
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The World Trade Organization (WTO) Fish Fund has officially extended the deadline for its second Call for Proposals to 8 May 2026, offering eligible countries additional time to submit applications for critical funding support. This extension provides a significant opportunity for developing and least-developed WTO member nations to access financial and technical assistance aimed at […]

The post WTO Fish Fund Extends Deadline to 8 May 2026: Expanded Opportunity for Developing Nations to Access Up to USD 300,000 in Grants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Trade Organization (WTO) Fish Fund has officially extended the deadline for its second Call for Proposals to 8 May 2026, offering eligible countries additional time to submit applications for critical funding support. This extension provides a significant opportunity for developing and least-developed WTO member nations to access financial and technical assistance aimed at implementing the Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies.

Overview of the WTO Fish Fund Call for Proposals

Originally launched on 2 February 2026, the second Call for Proposals invites applications from WTO members that meet specific eligibility criteria. The initiative is part of a broader global effort to promote sustainable fishing practices and ensure effective implementation of international fisheries agreements.

The Fish Fund operates as a targeted support mechanism to assist countries in strengthening their capacity, improving governance, and aligning national policies with global fisheries standards.

Purpose of the Fish Fund

The WTO Fish Fund was established under Article 7 of the Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies with a clear mission:

  • To provide technical assistance
  • To support capacity-building initiatives
  • To enable effective implementation of fisheries subsidy disciplines

This initiative plays a crucial role in addressing harmful fishing subsidies that contribute to overfishing and environmental degradation.

 Types of Grants Available

Applicants can apply for two distinct types of funding:

1. Project Preparation Grants

  • Maximum funding: USD 50,000
  • Purpose:
    • Conduct needs assessments
    • Prepare implementation strategies
    • Develop project proposals for future execution

2. Project Implementation Grants

  • Maximum funding: USD 300,000
  • Purpose:
    • Execute specific provisions of the Agreement
    • Strengthen national systems and compliance mechanisms
    • Support sustainable fisheries practices

 Previous Funding Outcomes

The first Call for Proposals demonstrated strong global interest and impact:

  • 26 project grants approved
  • Total funding allocated: Nearly USD 3 million
  • Approved in November 2025 by the Fish Fund Steering Committee

These results highlight the fund’s growing importance in supporting fisheries governance worldwide.

 Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for this funding opportunity:

  • Applicants must be WTO members
  • Must be classified as:
    • Developing countries, or
    • Least-developed countries (LDCs)
  • Must have formally accepted the Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies by depositing their instrument of acceptance

As of 26 March 2026, the total number of WTO members that have accepted the Agreement has reached 119, following recent acceptances by:

  • Paraguay
  • Samoa
  • Saint Vincent and the Grenadines

 Institutional Partnerships

The Fish Fund is hosted by the WTO and operates in collaboration with leading international organizations:

  • Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO)
  • International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD)
  • World Bank Group

These partnerships ensure that funded projects benefit from global expertise and technical support.

 Contributions and Funding Pool

The Fish Fund continues to receive strong financial backing from member countries:

  • Total contributions and commitments: CHF 15.7 million
  • Equivalent to over USD 20 million
  • Contributions from 18 WTO members, including:
    • Australia
    • Canada
    • European Union
    • France
    • Germany
    • Japan
    • United Kingdom
    • And several others

This robust funding pool ensures sustainability and scalability of supported projects.

 Application Deadline

All eligible WTO members must submit their proposals by:

8 May 2026

Late submissions will not be considered, making it essential for applicants to complete and submit their proposals before the deadline.

 How to Apply

Applicants can access the official application portal to submit their proposals and find additional guidance on the process. Interested parties are encouraged to begin preparations early to ensure a strong and complete application.

 Why This Opportunity Matters

The WTO Fish Fund represents a unique chance for developing nations to:

  • Strengthen fisheries governance
  • Promote environmental sustainability
  • Access international funding and expertise
  • Align with global trade and environmental standards

With the deadline now extended, more countries have the opportunity to participate and benefit from this impactful initiative.

For more information about this opportunity visit here.

Discover more global grant opportunities here.

The post WTO Fish Fund Extends Deadline to 8 May 2026: Expanded Opportunity for Developing Nations to Access Up to USD 300,000 in Grants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Rainforest Investigations Network Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Investigative Journalists Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Pulitzer Center is now accepting applications for the sixth cohort of its prestigious Rainforest Investigations Network (RIN) Fellowship 2026. This highly competitive, fully funded, yearlong program supports experienced investigative journalists working on critical environmental stories tied to deforestation, climate change, corruption, and governance across the world’s three major tropical rainforest regions: The Amazon The […]

The post Rainforest Investigations Network Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Investigative Journalists Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Pulitzer Center is now accepting applications for the sixth cohort of its prestigious Rainforest Investigations Network (RIN) Fellowship 2026. This highly competitive, fully funded, yearlong program supports experienced investigative journalists working on critical environmental stories tied to deforestation, climate change, corruption, and governance across the world’s three major tropical rainforest regions:

  • The Amazon
  • The Congo Basin
  • Southeast Asia

Application Deadline: May 22, 2026 (11:59 PM EST)

About the Rainforest Investigations Network (RIN)

The Rainforest Investigations Network is a global initiative designed to strengthen cross-border investigative journalism focused on uncovering the root causes of rainforest destruction. The fellowship enables journalists to explore:

  • Financial incentives behind deforestation
  • Illegal activities and environmental crimes
  • Global supply chains contributing to forest degradation
  • Governance failures and corruption

RIN Fellows collaborate across countries and newsrooms, producing impactful stories that reveal hidden systems driving environmental harm.

What the Fellowship Offers

Selected fellows will receive comprehensive support, including:

Financial Support

  • Coverage of salary (full or partial) depending on experience and location
  • Funding for reporting expenses, including:
    • Travel
    • Research
    • Hiring consultants

Training & Mentorship

  • Editorial guidance from a multidisciplinary team
  • Training in:
    • Investigative techniques
    • Data analysis
    • Satellite imagery tools
    • Corporate and financial investigations

 Global Collaboration

  • Work alongside a network of international investigative journalists
  • Opportunities to collaborate on cross-border investigations
  • Access to data, documents, and exclusive reporting resources

 Outreach & Engagement

  • Support from engagement teams to:
    • Share findings with communities
    • Present work in schools and universities
    • Reach global audiences

 What the Program is Looking For

The RIN Fellowship seeks seasoned investigative journalists who:

  • Have a strong track record in investigative reporting
  • Focus on or are based in rainforest regions
  • Can propose ambitious, original, and impactful investigations
  • Are open to collaboration and teamwork
  • Use innovative, data-driven approaches

Priority Areas

Special emphasis is placed on projects that:

  • Take a cross-border investigative approach
  • Examine systemic drivers of deforestation
  • Investigate financial systems and global supply chains

Transparency & Governance Track

Two fellowship slots are reserved for projects focusing on:

  • Financial institutions enabling environmental destruction
  • Corporate accountability
  • Legal loopholes and governance failures
  • Corruption linked to deforestation

 Examples of Past Investigations

Previous fellows have worked on impactful stories such as:

  • Mining linked to electric vehicle supply chains
  • Corporate pollution in the Amazon
  • Financial flows into agribusiness driving deforestation
  • Carbon offset projects with questionable claims
  • Government-backed illegal deforestation initiatives

 Fellowship Commitment

Fellows are expected to:

  • Work full-time for one year on their investigation
  • Publish their stories during the fellowship period
  • Participate in:
    • Regular meetings
    • Training sessions
    • Peer collaboration

They must also maintain transparency and actively contribute to the network’s collaborative environment.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be experienced investigative journalists
  • Work as staff reporters or freelancers (with a hosting newsroom)
  • Have a strong understanding of environmental, political, and economic issues
  • Be able to collaborate across borders and teams
  • Commit to outreach and public engagement activities

Journalists outside rainforest regions can apply but must focus their reporting on those regions.

 Application Requirements

Applicants must submit:

  1. Statement of Purpose (500 words)
    • Career goals and fit for the fellowship
    • Collaboration potential
  2. Investigative Project Proposal (1,000 words)
    • A detailed, original, and well-researched story idea
    • Must demonstrate feasibility and impact
  3. Publication & Audience Plan (500 words)
    • Distribution strategy
    • Target audience and engagement approach
  4. Three Published Investigations (from the past 3 years)
  5. Letter of Support from a newsroom
  6. Three Professional References
  7. Resume/CV

 Application Languages

Applications are accepted in:

  • English
  • Portuguese
  • Spanish
  • French
  • Bahasa Indonesia

 Ask Me Anything (AMA) Sessions

Applicants can join live sessions with editors in multiple languages:

  • English: April 28, 2026
  • Bahasa Indonesia: April 29, 2026
  • Spanish: April 30, 2026
  • Portuguese: May 5, 2026
  • French: May 7, 2026

 How to Apply

Submit your application via the official form before the deadline:

Deadline: May 22, 2026 (11:59 PM EST)

For inquiries: rin@pulitzercenter.org

 Why This Fellowship Matters

The destruction of tropical rainforests is one of the most urgent global challenges. This fellowship empowers journalists to uncover hidden truths, expose powerful systems, and drive accountability on a global scale.

For more information about this opportunity visit here.

Discover more fellowship programs here.

 

The post Rainforest Investigations Network Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Investigative Journalists Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Data Governance for Inclusive Digital & AI Futures: Free Global Online Course by ITU, UNDP & UNESCO (2026)
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The rapid evolution of digital technologies and artificial intelligence has made data governance a critical priority for governments, institutions, and development practitioners worldwide. To address this need, leading international organizations have launched a comprehensive training program titled “Data Governance for Inclusive Digital & AI Futures.” This course is designed to equip participants with the knowledge, […]

The post Data Governance for Inclusive Digital & AI Futures: Free Global Online Course by ITU, UNDP & UNESCO (2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The rapid evolution of digital technologies and artificial intelligence has made data governance a critical priority for governments, institutions, and development practitioners worldwide. To address this need, leading international organizations have launched a comprehensive training program titled “Data Governance for Inclusive Digital & AI Futures.” This course is designed to equip participants with the knowledge, frameworks, and practical tools needed to build inclusive, secure, and rights-based data systems.

Offered as a fully funded training opportunity, this course provides a unique chance for professionals across the globe to strengthen their understanding of how data can drive equitable digital transformation.

Overview of the Course

This online instructor-led training program focuses on building foundational and practical expertise in data governance. It emphasizes the importance of creating trustworthy data ecosystems that support innovation while safeguarding rights and ensuring inclusivity.

Key Details:

  • Registration Period: 21 April 2026 – 31 May 2026 (Deadline)
  • Course Dates: 23 June 2026 – 21 July 2026
  • Location: Global (Online)
  • Duration: 12 hours
  • Course Level: Intermediate
  • Language: English
  • Cost: Free of charge for selected applicants
  • Training Type: Online instructor-led

Organizers and Partners

This course is jointly organized by globally recognized institutions working at the intersection of technology, development, and policy:

  • International Telecommunication Union (ITU)
  • United Nations Development Programme (UNDP)
  • UNESCO

The program is supported by the European Union’s Global Gateway initiative, ensuring accessibility for participants from diverse backgrounds.

Course Description

The course offers a deep dive into the principles and practices of data governance in the digital age. Participants will explore how to design and implement frameworks that enable:

  • Secure and ethical data use
  • Inclusive digital transformation
  • Evidence-based policymaking

The curriculum integrates insights from the Data Governance Toolkit, developed collaboratively by UNESCO, UNDP, ITU, and the African Union Commission under the Broadband Commission Working Group.

What You Will Learn

Participants will gain both theoretical understanding and practical insights, including:

  1. Policy Dimensions of Data Governance
    • Designing regulatory frameworks
    • Aligning with national digital strategies and SDGs
  2. Institutional Frameworks
    • Governance structures for data management
    • Stakeholder coordination and accountability
  3. Technical Foundations
    • Data quality, interoperability, and transparency
    • Building secure and trusted data ecosystems
  4. Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI)
    • Governance components of DPI
    • Role of data in public service delivery
  5. AI and Data Ecosystems
    • Importance of reliable data for AI systems
    • Safeguards for ethical AI deployment
  6. Global Case Studies
    • Country experiences and best practices
    • Actionable strategies for implementation

Learning Approach

The course uses an interactive and practical methodology to enhance engagement and knowledge retention:

  • Live instructor-led sessions
  • Case studies and real-world examples
  • Peer discussions and collaborative learning
  • Practical tools for policymaking

Target Audience

This course is ideal for:

  • Policymakers
  • Government officials
  • Digital transformation practitioners
  • Data governance professionals
  • Development sector experts

It is particularly relevant for those involved in shaping digital policies, managing data systems, or working on AI-related initiatives.

Tutors and Coordinators

Participants will learn from experienced experts in the field:

  • Tutors: Leona Verdadero, Christophe Ji
  • Coordinators: Queenie Tam, Ghazi Mabrouk, Angel Draev

Why You Should Apply

This course offers several valuable benefits:

  • Gain globally recognized expertise in data governance
  • Learn directly from international organizations
  • Access practical tools and frameworks for real-world application
  • Understand the intersection of data, AI, and public policy
  • Network with professionals from around the world
  • Participate at no cost if selected

Inclusivity and Accessibility

The program strongly promotes diversity and inclusion. Applications are encouraged from:

  • Women professionals
  • Participants from developing countries
  • Individuals from least developed countries, small island developing states, and landlocked developing countries

The course is open to all professionals regardless of background, ensuring equal access to learning opportunities.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should complete their registration before the deadline:

  • Deadline: 31 May 2026

Applicants may also refer to the official registration document for detailed instructions and requirements.

Final Thoughts

As data becomes the backbone of digital economies and AI systems, understanding how to govern it responsibly is no longer optional—it is essential. This course provides a timely opportunity to build expertise that can shape inclusive and sustainable digital futures.

Visit  here for more information and to apply

Discover more global courses on OFY  here. 

 

The post Data Governance for Inclusive Digital & AI Futures: Free Global Online Course by ITU, UNDP & UNESCO (2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Comprehensive Security Fellowship in Bonn 2027/28 – Academic & Practitioner Tracks on International Politics, Strategic Resilience, and Global Security Challenges
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
Comprehensive Security Fellowship Call 2027/28 – AIA NRW (Bonn, Germany) The Academy in Bonn (AIA NRW) has officially opened its Fellowship Call for the 2027/28 cohort, inviting applications from scholars and practitioners working in the broad field of international politics, security studies, and strategic resilience. This prestigious fellowship focuses on rethinking global security in an […]

The post Comprehensive Security Fellowship in Bonn 2027/28 – Academic & Practitioner Tracks on International Politics, Strategic Resilience, and Global Security Challenges appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Comprehensive Security Fellowship Call 2027/28 – AIA NRW (Bonn, Germany)

The Academy in Bonn (AIA NRW) has officially opened its Fellowship Call for the 2027/28 cohort, inviting applications from scholars and practitioners working in the broad field of international politics, security studies, and strategic resilience. This prestigious fellowship focuses on rethinking global security in an era marked by geopolitical uncertainty, technological transformation, and evolving international alliances.

The program emphasizes the concept of “Comprehensive Security for a Changing World: Building Strategic and Technological Resilience”, reflecting Europe’s shifting security landscape shaped by open conflicts, global competition, and institutional challenges.

About the Fellowship Program

This fellowship is designed to bring together interdisciplinary researchers and professionals to explore how modern security frameworks can address interconnected global risks.

Key focus areas include:

  • International security and defence cooperation
  • Governance frameworks in global politics
  • Technological governance and digital transformation
  • Supply chain security and economic resilience
  • Protection of democratic institutions
  • Strategic foresight and global risk analysis
  • Emerging geopolitical and security threats

The program supports innovative research and practice-oriented reflection that contributes to both academic discourse and real-world policy solutions.

Fellowship Tracks

Applicants can apply under one of two distinct tracks:

1. Academic Track

This track is intended for candidates with a strong research background.

It requires:

  • A clear academic research focus within international politics or security studies
  • Engagement with existing or emerging scholarly debates
  • A defined contribution to academic knowledge and theory-building

2. Practitioner’s Track

This track is designed for professionals working in relevant fields.

It requires:

  • Reflection on international politics from practical or policy experience
  • Integration of real-world professional insights
  • Contribution to applied understanding of global security challenges

Fellowship Expectations and Responsibilities

Selected fellows are expected to actively participate in the intellectual life of the Academy in Bonn. Responsibilities include:

  • Full-time residence in Bonn during the fellowship period
  • Regular participation in seminars, colloquia, and academic discussions
  • Contribution to workshops and institutional events
  • Engagement in publication activities and research outputs
  • Willingness to support knowledge transfer and public engagement initiatives
  • Optional involvement in social media and outreach activities

Fellowship Period

  • Duration: April 2027 – March 2028
  • Fellows must be present in Bonn throughout the program without extended absences

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit a single PDF file including the following documents:

Mandatory Documents

  1. Letter of motivation
  2. Research/project proposal (maximum 5 pages), including:
    • Abstract
    • Research questions or guiding objectives
    • State of research overview
    • Detailed work plan and timeline
    • Expected outputs
    • Added value of conducting research at AIA NRW in Bonn
  3. Short CV (maximum 2 pages)

Additional Requirements for Academic Track Applicants

  1. Certified PhD certificate
  2. Full list of academic publications
  3. 2–3 recent peer-reviewed journal articles (in English)

Key Dates and Deadlines

  • Application Deadline: 31 May 2026
  • Decision Announcement: Mid-September 2026
  • Fellowship Period: April 2027 – March 2028

Selection Process

Applications will be reviewed by the Academic Board, which will evaluate submissions based on academic quality, relevance to the fellowship theme, and potential contribution to international political and security discourse.

Additional Information

Applicants are encouraged to:

  • Demonstrate innovative thinking in addressing global security challenges
  • Highlight interdisciplinary approaches
  • Show relevance to contemporary geopolitical transformations
  • Engage with policy, academic, or strategic communities

Contact Information

Dr. Katja Freistein
Academic Coordinator, Fellowship Programme
AIA NRW (Bonn, Germany)

Apply here

For more information about this opportunity visit here.

Discover more fellowship programs here.

The post Comprehensive Security Fellowship in Bonn 2027/28 – Academic & Practitioner Tracks on International Politics, Strategic Resilience, and Global Security Challenges appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: SIPRI Research Assistant Job Opportunity in Sweden (2026) – Work in Global Security & WMD Research
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) has opened applications for a highly competitive Research Assistant position within its Armament and Disarmament Cluster, specifically under the Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD) Programme. This is an exceptional opportunity for individuals passionate about global security, arms control, and international peacebuilding to work at one of the world’s […]

The post SIPRI Research Assistant Job Opportunity in Sweden (2026) – Work in Global Security & WMD Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI) has opened applications for a highly competitive Research Assistant position within its Armament and Disarmament Cluster, specifically under the Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD) Programme. This is an exceptional opportunity for individuals passionate about global security, arms control, and international peacebuilding to work at one of the world’s most respected research institutions.

Located in Solna, Stockholm (Sweden), this full-time role offers not only professional growth but also the chance to contribute meaningfully to global discussions on disarmament, non-proliferation, and emerging security threats.

 About SIPRI

Founded in 1966, SIPRI is an independent international institute dedicated to:

  • Research on conflict, security, and peace
  • Providing data-driven analysis and policy recommendations
  • Supporting governments, researchers, media, and the public

SIPRI is globally recognized for its neutrality, academic excellence, and impactful contributions to international peace and security discourse.

 About the WMD Programme

The Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD) Programme focuses on analyzing:

  • Nuclear, chemical, and biological weapons
  • Outer space security and capabilities
  • Arms control and disarmament frameworks
  • Non-proliferation strategies
  • Risk reduction in global security systems

The programme combines political, legal, institutional, and technical research to address complex global challenges.

 Position Overview: Research Assistant

The Research Assistant will support SIPRI’s WMD Programme in delivering high-quality research and operational outputs.

Key Responsibilities

  • Conduct research collection and analytical support for WMD-related projects
  • Assist in organizing and contributing to:
    • Publications
    • Workshops
    • Meetings and events
  • Support administrative and substantive project implementation
  • Perform fact-checking, proofreading, and reviewing of publications (including the SIPRI Yearbook)
  • Collaborate with team members and contribute to the institute’s academic environment

Requirements and Qualifications

Applicants must demonstrate a strong academic and professional foundation in relevant areas.

Educational Background

  • A university degree in a relevant discipline such as:
    • International Relations
    • Security Studies
    • Political Science
    • Science/Technology fields related to WMD or space

Skills and Experience

  • Knowledge or research experience in:
    • Weapons of mass destruction (WMD)
    • Outer space security
  • Familiarity with:
    • Arms control
    • Non-proliferation
    • Disarmament policies
  • Interest in multilateral institutions and global governance

Core Competencies

  • Strong attention to detail
  • Excellent organizational and time management skills
  • Ability to work independently and in teams
  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite
  • Fluency in written and spoken English

Location & Work Conditions

  • Location: Solna, Stockholm, Sweden
  • Employment Type: Full-time
  • Residency Requirement: Must relocate to Sweden upon employment
  • SIPRI provides:
    • Support for work and residence permits
    • A collaborative and international work environment

Contract Duration

  • Initial contract: Up to 2 years
  • Possible extension: Up to 6 years total

Salary & Benefits

  • Salary is negotiated individually
  • Additional benefits include:
    • Professional development opportunities
    • Exposure to global policy networks
    • Work in a prestigious international research institute

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following documents in English:

  1. One-page cover letter
    • Clearly explain your qualifications
    • State why you are applying
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
    • Include:
      • Education
      • Work experience
      • Publications (if any)
      • Language skills
      • Two referees
  3. Writing Sample
    • Maximum of 10 pages
    • Relevant to the position

 Application Deadline

  • Closing Date: 22 May 2026
  • Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis until the position is filled
  • Interviews are expected to take place in June 2026

Contact Information

For inquiries about the role, applicants can contact:

  • Dr. Wilfred Wan
    Senior Researcher and Director, WMD Programme

 How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply through the official SIPRI application portal: Apply here

 Equal Opportunity Statement

SIPRI is committed to diversity and inclusion:

  • Encourages applications from women and underrepresented groups
  • Welcomes candidates from all backgrounds and nationalities

For more information about this opportunity visit here.

Discover more jobs  here.

The post SIPRI Research Assistant Job Opportunity in Sweden (2026) – Work in Global Security & WMD Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: MIT Solve 10th Anniversary Global Challenge 2026: Funding Scalable Innovations for Global Impact Across Health, Climate, Education, and Economic Opportunity
item.dc:creator: Sarah Kayiwa
item.description:
The MIT Solve 10th Anniversary Global Challenge 2026 marks a major milestone in global innovation, celebrating a decade of identifying, funding, and scaling impactful solutions to some of the world’s most urgent challenges. This initiative is designed to support visionary innovators who are building durable, scalable, and equitable solutions that can operate effectively over the […]

The post MIT Solve 10th Anniversary Global Challenge 2026: Funding Scalable Innovations for Global Impact Across Health, Climate, Education, and Economic Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The MIT Solve 10th Anniversary Global Challenge 2026 marks a major milestone in global innovation, celebrating a decade of identifying, funding, and scaling impactful solutions to some of the world’s most urgent challenges. This initiative is designed to support visionary innovators who are building durable, scalable, and equitable solutions that can operate effectively over the next decade and beyond.

MIT Solve is built on a simple but powerful belief: the best solutions to global problems already exist—but they need to be discovered, supported, and scaled responsibly. Over the past ten years, this platform has helped innovators reach hundreds of millions of lives and mobilize over a billion dollars in funding and support.

This 10th anniversary edition is not just a competition—it is a global call to action for solutions that can reshape systems and deliver long-term impact.

About the MIT Solve 10th Anniversary Global Challenge

This global challenge seeks 10 exceptional solutions that can demonstrate strong potential for scale, sustainability, and measurable impact. Selected innovators—referred to as “Solvers”—will join a global network of partners, funders, and institutions committed to advancing real-world change.

The focus is on innovations that are not only technically strong but also:

  • Socially inclusive
  • Economically viable
  • Politically and culturally adaptable
  • Built for long-term implementation

The challenge emphasizes solutions that can be adopted widely through markets, policy systems, procurement pipelines, or digital platforms.

Key Global Problem Areas for 2026

Applicants are encouraged to propose solutions across the following priority areas:

1. Climate Action and Environmental Sustainability

  • Building a zero-carbon and climate-resilient world
  • Supporting communities already affected by climate change
  • Scalable adaptation solutions for vulnerable regions
  • Innovations that reduce emissions and improve environmental resilience

2. Economic Opportunity and Financial Inclusion

  • Expanding jobs and sustainable livelihoods
  • Supporting small business growth and entrepreneurship
  • Improving access to financial systems and tools
  • Strengthening economic mobility in underserved populations

3. Health Systems Strengthening

  • Improving healthcare delivery systems
  • Expanding access to quality care and medical workforce support
  • Strengthening supply chains and prevention systems
  • Enhancing health resilience against global shocks

4. Learning and Education for All

  • Delivering measurable learning improvements
  • Supporting learners with diverse learning needs
  • Innovating modern education systems and tools
  • Expanding equitable access to quality education globally

5. Indigenous Communities and Sovereignty

  • Supporting Indigenous-led innovation
  • Strengthening social, environmental, and economic sovereignty
  • Promoting culturally grounded solutions
  • Advancing self-determined development pathways

Why This Challenge Matters

Over the last decade, global systems have been tested by:

  • Public health crises and pandemics
  • Climate-related disasters and environmental instability
  • Rising inequality and economic disruption
  • Rapid advances in AI and digital technologies

MIT Solve recognizes that innovation alone is not enough. Solutions must be:

  • Scalable (able to grow across regions and populations)
  • Durable (sustainable over time)
  • Trust-based (ethical and inclusive in design and implementation)

Through this challenge, MIT Solve aims to support innovations that not only solve problems but also rebuild broken systems and strengthen global resilience.

What Selected Solvers Receive

Winners and selected innovators gain access to:

  • Strategic funding opportunities
  • Global visibility and recognition
  • Technical and implementation support
  • Partnerships with governments, NGOs, and private sector leaders
  • Integration into MIT Solve’s global innovation ecosystem

Important Dates (2026 Timeline)

  • Applications Open: March 16, 2026
  • Submission Deadline: May 21, 2026 (12:00pm EDT)
  • Solution Deadline: May 21, 2026 (12:00pm EDT)
  • Semifinalist Selection: June 25, 2026
  • Finalist Selection: September 22, 2026
  • Winner Announcement: Date not specified

Final Objective of the Challenge

MIT Solve is actively seeking innovations that:

  • Address urgent global challenges
  • Can be implemented at scale
  • Deliver measurable social impact
  • Strengthen systems rather than temporary fixes
  • Combine technology with trust, equity, and sustainability

This is a defining opportunity for innovators who want to shape the next decade of global problem-solving.

For more information about this opportunity visit here. 

Discover more grants here.

The post MIT Solve 10th Anniversary Global Challenge 2026: Funding Scalable Innovations for Global Impact Across Health, Climate, Education, and Economic Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: VV Venture: AI for Impact 2026 — Global Program for Women Social Entrepreneurs
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for VV Venture: AI for Impact, a global program designed to empower women social entrepreneurs to scale their ventures using artificial intelligence. This initiative targets established founders who are building purpose-driven businesses and are ready to integrate AI into their operations, customer engagement, and decision-making processes. About the Program VV […]

The post VV Venture: AI for Impact 2026 — Global Program for Women Social Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for VV Venture: AI for Impact, a global program designed to empower women social entrepreneurs to scale their ventures using artificial intelligence.

This initiative targets established founders who are building purpose-driven businesses and are ready to integrate AI into their operations, customer engagement, and decision-making processes.

About the Program

VV Venture: AI for Impact combines leadership development with practical, real-world applications of AI. The program is designed to help participants strengthen their ventures while connecting with a global network of like-minded entrepreneurs and experts.

Participants will gain hands-on experience in applying AI tools to improve efficiency, enhance customer relationships, and make data-driven decisions that support sustainable growth.

Key Benefits

This program offers a unique blend of learning and growth opportunities:

  • Leadership development tailored for women entrepreneurs
  • Practical AI application for business growth
  • Improved operational efficiency and decision-making
  • Strategies to deepen customer engagement
  • Access to a global network of founders and experts

Why You Should Apply

As AI continues to transform industries worldwide, entrepreneurs who understand how to leverage it effectively will gain a significant competitive advantage.

This program is particularly valuable because it does not require a technical background, making it accessible to founders who want to adopt AI without needing coding or engineering skills.

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is ideal for:

  • Women social entrepreneurs
  • Founders of purpose-driven ventures
  • Established business owners looking to scale
  • Entrepreneurs interested in applying AI to real-world challenges

Applicants should demonstrate a clear mission, a growing venture, and a strong desire to innovate and scale impact.

Application Deadline

No specific deadline has been announced. Applications are likely reviewed on a rolling basis, so early application is strongly encouraged.

How to Apply

Apply here.

For more Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post VV Venture: AI for Impact 2026 — Global Program for Women Social Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: European Commission Call for Proposals 2026: Strengthening Human Rights, Democracy, and ESG Accountability in South Africa
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The European Commission has officially launched a major funding opportunity aimed at empowering civil society organisations (CSOs) in South Africa. This call for proposals focuses on strengthening human rights, promoting democratic governance, and advancing Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) accountability—particularly in the context of a just and inclusive transition. With a substantial budget allocation of […]

The post European Commission Call for Proposals 2026: Strengthening Human Rights, Democracy, and ESG Accountability in South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Commission has officially launched a major funding opportunity aimed at empowering civil society organisations (CSOs) in South Africa. This call for proposals focuses on strengthening human rights, promoting democratic governance, and advancing Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) accountability—particularly in the context of a just and inclusive transition.

With a substantial budget allocation of €5,164,000, this initiative represents a significant investment in fostering inclusive development, civic engagement, and responsible governance practices across South Africa.

 

Overview of the Opportunity

  • Programme: Multi-programme framework
  • Geographical Focus: South Africa
  • Funding Type: Action Grants
  • Total Budget: €5,164,000
  • Publication Date: 24 April 2026
  • Deadline for Applications: 23 July 2026

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparations early to ensure a strong and competitive submission.

 

Purpose of the Call

This call is designed to support projects that:

  1. Strengthen Human Rights Frameworks
    • Promote the protection and realization of fundamental human rights.
    • Support advocacy, monitoring, and accountability mechanisms.
  2. Enhance Democratic Governance
    • Encourage citizen participation in governance processes.
    • Strengthen transparency, accountability, and institutional integrity.
  3. Promote ESG Accountability
    • Improve corporate and public sector accountability in environmental, social, and governance practices.
    • Encourage sustainable and ethical business conduct.
  4. Support a Just Transition
    • Facilitate inclusive economic transitions that consider social equity.
    • Ensure vulnerable and marginalized populations are not left behind in sustainability efforts.

 

Key Priority Areas

Successful proposals are expected to align with one or more of the following thematic priorities:

  • Civil Society Empowerment
    • Capacity-building for CSOs to effectively engage in governance and policy dialogue.
  • Human Rights Advocacy
    • Projects that address inequality, discrimination, and social injustice.
  • Environmental and Social Governance (ESG)
    • Initiatives that monitor and promote ESG standards in both public and private sectors.
  • Inclusive Economic Transition
    • Supporting communities impacted by shifts toward greener economies.

 

Funding Scope and Activities

The grant supports a wide range of activities, including but not limited to:

  • Policy advocacy and reform initiatives
  • Capacity-building workshops and training programs
  • Research and data collection on governance and ESG issues
  • Community engagement and awareness campaigns
  • Monitoring and evaluation of public policies and corporate practices

Projects should demonstrate sustainability, scalability, and measurable impact.

 

Why This Opportunity Matters

This initiative is particularly important as it:

  • Addresses pressing governance and human rights challenges in South Africa
  • Encourages collaboration between civil society, government, and private sector actors
  • Supports global commitments to sustainable development and climate justice
  • Strengthens accountability systems that benefit both citizens and institutions

 

Application Process

Interested applicants must submit their proposals through the official EU Funding & Tenders Portal.

Applicants should carefully review all documentation, eligibility criteria, and submission guidelines before applying.

 

Tips for a Strong Application

  • Clearly align your project with the call’s objectives
  • Demonstrate strong impact and sustainability
  • Include measurable indicators and realistic timelines
  • Highlight partnerships and stakeholder engagement
  • Ensure compliance with EU funding requirements

 

Conclusion

This European Commission call presents a powerful opportunity for civil society organisations to drive meaningful change in South Africa. By focusing on human rights, democratic governance, and ESG accountability, funded projects will contribute to a more inclusive, transparent, and sustainable future.

Organisations working in these areas should take full advantage of this opportunity to scale their impact and influence policy at both local and national levels.

 

Apply here

Learn More

  • Details about this opportunity: Click here
  • More grant opportunities for youth: Visit the OFY website: Click here

The post European Commission Call for Proposals 2026: Strengthening Human Rights, Democracy, and ESG Accountability in South Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Gender and Learning Evidence Fund 2026 | Up to $250,000 Grants for Education Research in Africa
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Gender and Learning Evidence Fund is now open for applications, offering grants of up to $250,000 USD to organisations working to improve education outcomes while addressing gender gaps across Africa. Supported by the Brink Foundation with funding from the Gates Foundation, this opportunity is designed for organisations already implementing education or gender-focused programmes that […]

The post Gender and Learning Evidence Fund 2026 | Up to $250,000 Grants for Education Research in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Gender and Learning Evidence Fund is now open for applications, offering grants of up to $250,000 USD to organisations working to improve education outcomes while addressing gender gaps across Africa. Supported by the Brink Foundation with funding from the Gates Foundation, this opportunity is designed for organisations already implementing education or gender-focused programmes that want to generate stronger evidence, test new approaches, and improve long-term outcomes for girls and boys.

This funding call is especially relevant for organisations working in schools during critical transition years, where many students begin to face barriers to progression. Selected grantees will receive funding for implementation research, technical assistance, and access to a collaborative learning cohort.

About the Program

The Gender and Learning Evidence Fund is based on the idea that learning outcomes and gender equality should be addressed together rather than separately. Many education programmes focus only on academic progress, while gender programmes often overlook learning outcomes. This fund aims to bridge that gap.

The program will support 6 to 8 grants for organisations conducting implementation research in real-world settings. Rather than funding theoretical studies, the fund is looking for practical projects that test what works in classrooms, schools, and communities.

Projects can focus on improving learning for girls, boys, or both, while also measuring broader outcomes such as school progression, aspirations, empowerment, and wellbeing.

Funding is available for projects lasting up to 24 months.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Across many countries, gender gaps in education often widen during adolescence, especially when students transition from primary to secondary school. At the same time, learning levels remain a major concern.

This fund recognises that improving literacy, numeracy, and school quality can also improve long-term outcomes such as employment, health, and empowerment. It also recognises that girls and boys may face different barriers in school and need better-targeted support.

For organisations already working in these areas, this is a valuable chance to strengthen evidence, improve programme design, and influence future policy and investment decisions.

What Participants Will Gain

Selected organisations will receive up to $250,000 USD in grant funding to support research and programme improvements.

Beyond funding, grantees will also gain access to technical support in areas such as research design, monitoring and evaluation, data analysis, adaptive testing, and policy engagement.

Participants will join a cohort of organisations tackling similar challenges, creating opportunities for peer learning and collaboration.

The Brink Foundation will also support grantees in communicating results through publications, evidence briefs, and wider visibility opportunities.

For organisations seeking to scale impact or improve government uptake, this added support can be just as valuable as the funding itself.

Who Can Apply

Applications are open to registered organisations from multiple sectors, including nonprofits, academic institutions, for-profits, multilateral bodies, faith-based organisations, and government agencies.

To be eligible, applicants must already be implementing a programme focused on learning, gender equality, or both.

Projects must focus on students who are currently enrolled in school and should target one or both of these transition stages:

Grades 4 to 6 (lower to upper primary)
Grades 7 to 9 (upper primary to secondary)

Consortia and multi-country proposals are also welcome where feasible.

Eligible Areas

Applications are open to organisations registered in the following countries:

Côte d’Ivoire, Ethiopia, Ghana, Kenya, Mozambique, Nigeria, Senegal, South Africa, and Tanzania.

Projects may operate in one or multiple eligible countries.

Application Process and Deadline

The application process is intentionally light-touch. Applicants are required to submit a short two-page proposal outlining their current programme, the proposed research project, and expected impact.

Shortlisted applicants will then participate in two interview stages.

The application deadline is:

5 June 2026 at 11:59 PM CAT

Selected organisations are expected to begin onboarding from August 2026.

Organisations working at the intersection of education quality, gender equality, and evidence-based programming should strongly consider applying.

Learn more and apply on the official Brink Foundation website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Gender and Learning Evidence Fund 2026 | Up to $250,000 Grants for Education Research in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
